_comment: The 11-age arc per CONCEPT.md §3.5. All names are the canon list; era descriptors are flavor hooks mapping each age to a real-world/mythic era per PM's 'Adam and Eve → 2026' directive. Adam-and-Eve framing is Primal-age lore only; age names themselves remain canon per §5.4. All stats-driving fields live in towers.json + enemies.json; this file is theming. Port target: Godot 4 Resource array (res://data/ages/*.tres). All [PROPOSAL] until playtest ratifies.

ages.name: Primal
ages.eraDescriptor: Age of Creation — Eden and the Garden
ages.signatureMotif: muddy earth, wooden stakes, raw sinew
ages.lore: First light on first earth. The Commander places the first stone where the first garden grew. Everything after is built on what this age laid down.

ages.name: Mudbrick
ages.signatureMotif: sun-baked walls, ziggurats, reed and clay
ages.lore: The walls stop being hide and start being earth. Towers remember this age as the first time a thing could be *rebuilt* where it fell.

ages.name: Iron
ages.eraDescriptor: Iron Age — Greek polis, Persia, Han
ages.signatureMotif: forged blades, marble columns, disciplined lines
ages.lore: Order, geometry, the column. What the mudbrick age raised, the iron age squared. Every marble colonnade is a descendant of a first straight line drawn here.

ages.name: Spires
ages.eraDescriptor: Medieval — Gothic cathedrals and stone keeps
ages.lore: Height as ambition. Every tower in this age wants to be *taller*, and the price is stonemasons who die old without seeing the spire they started.

ages.name: Alchemical
ages.eraDescriptor: Renaissance / Early Modern — gunpowder and discovery
ages.lore: Fire that can be *aimed*. Knowledge that can be *written down*. The first age where a tower's secret is not a craft but a formula.

ages.name: Steamheart
ages.signatureMotif: boilers, rivets, brass gauges under pressure
ages.lore: A tower now has a heartbeat, and the heartbeat is steam. This is the first age where a wall can *move*.

ages.name: Atomic
ages.eraDescriptor: Atomic Age — world wars, reactors, Sputnik
ages.signatureMotif: reactor cores, vacuum tubes, the mushroom shape
ages.lore: The tower learns the atom. What a wall could once contain, a tower now splits. Lineages that survive this age learn humility or they don't survive the next.

ages.name: Signal
ages.eraDescriptor: Information Age — satellites, fiber, early internet
ages.lore: The tower stops being where you stand and starts being what you *reach*. Towers in this age speak to each other across lanes.

ages.name: Synthetic
ages.signatureMotif: lab-grown lattice, liquid-cooled silicon, gene-edit scars
ages.lore: A tower can now be *designed*. The distance between the Commander's intent and the wall's shape collapses to a compile step.

ages.name: Stellar
ages.eraDescriptor: Beyond Earth — orbit, lunar, Mars analog
ages.signatureMotif: vacuum-welded lattice, ion exhaust, earthrise seen from the wall
ages.lore: A tower that does not need gravity. Walls built in silence, learning the old grammar of the first garden without the earth underneath.

ages.name: Ascendant
ages.signatureMotif: light-lattice, ideas that have been built, the altar
ages.lore: The last age names itself the first. Every tower built before is a sentence in the story told here. From ash to altar, the Commander's line becomes what it was always shaped toward.
